```python
                       ((1 + (q/p) * exp_term)**2)
        
        # Seasonal effects
        seasonal_multiplier = pm.Normal('seasonal_effect',
                                        mu=[1.3, 0.9, 1.25, 1.0],  # Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter
                                        sigma=0.1,
                                        shape=n_seasons)
        
        # Social media effect
        social_media_coefficient = pm.Normal('social_media_impact',
                                             mu=0.8,  # 0.8 correlation
                                             sigma=0.1)
        
        social_effect = 1 + social_media_coefficient * (social_media_mentions - 1)
        
        # Price elasticity
        price_elasticity = pm.Normal('price_elasticity',
                                     mu=-1.2,
                                     sigma=0.2)
        
        # Reference price by trend type
        reference_price = pm.Normal('reference_price',
                                    mu=[150, 80, 120],  # Emerging premium
                                    sigma=20,
                                    shape=n_types)
        
        price_effect = (price / reference_price[trend_type])**price_elasticity
        
        # Competition effect
        competition_factor = pm.Normal('competition_impact',
                                       mu=-0.05,  # -5% per competitor
                                       sigma=0.02)
        
        competition_effect = pm.math.exp(competition_factor * competitor_items)
        
        # Trend decay (fashion cycles)
        decay_rate = pm.HalfNormal('trend_decay_rate',
                                   sigma=[0.1, 0.05, 0.02],  # Fast to slow decay
                                   shape=n_types)
        
        # After peak, adoption decays
        weeks_after_peak = pm.math.maximum(0, weeks_since_launch - time_to_peak[trend_type])
        decay_effect = pm.math.exp(-decay_rate[trend_type] * weeks_after_peak)
        
        # Inventory effects (stockouts reduce sales)
        inventory_factor = pm.Beta('inventory_availability',
                                   alpha=9, beta=1)  # Usually ~0.9
        
        # Base demand model
        base_demand = adoption_rate * 10000  # Scale to units
        
        # Total demand
        expected_sales = (base_demand * 
                         seasonal_multiplier[season] * 
                         social_effect * 
                         price_effect * 
                         competition_effect * 
                         decay_effect * 
                         inventory_factor)
        
        # Ensure non-negative
        expected_sales_positive = pm.math.maximum(expected_sales, 0)
        
        # Observation model (Negative Binomial for count data)
        alpha = pm.Exponential('overdispersion', 0.5)
        
        sales_obs = pm.NegativeBinomial('sales_observed',
                                        mu=expected_sales_positive,
                                        alpha=alpha,
                                        observed=sales)
        
        # Trend lifecycle metrics
        # Total lifecycle sales
        total_lifecycle_sales = pm.Deterministic('total_lifecycle_units',
                                                 m * 10000 * inventory_factor)
        
        # Weeks to 80% of sales
        lifecycle_duration = pm.Deterministic('lifecycle_weeks_80pct',
                                              -pm.math.log(0.2) / (p + q) * 
                                              time_to_peak[trend_type])
        
        # Revenue optimization
        unit_cost = price * 0.4  # 60% margin
        revenue = expected_sales_positive * price
        profit = expected_sales_positive * (price - unit_cost)
        
        # Optimal price point
        # Derivative of profit w.r.t. price = 0
        optimal_price = pm.Deterministic('optimal_price',
                                         reference_price[trend_type] * 
                                         (-price_elasticity / (price_elasticity + 1)))
        
        # Trend classification metrics
        # Virality score (q/p ratio)
        virality = pm.Deterministic('virality_score',
                                    q / p)
        
        # Speed score (weeks to peak)
        speed_score = pm.Deterministic('trend_speed_score',
                                       1 / time_to_peak[trend_type])
        
        # Longevity score (inverse decay rate)
        longevity = pm.Deterministic('longevity_score',
                                     1 / decay_rate[trend_type])
        
        # Market share
        market_share = pm.Deterministic('market_share_pct',
                                        cumulative_adoption * 100)
        
        # Inventory planning
        # Safety stock based on demand variability
        cv = pm.math.sqrt(alpha) / expected_sales_positive  # Coefficient of variation
        safety_stock = pm.Deterministic('safety_stock_units',
                                        1.65 * pm.math.sqrt(expected_sales_positive) * cv)
        
        # Reorder point (2 week lead time)
        lead_time_demand = expected_sales_positive * 2
        reorder_point = pm.Deterministic('reorder_point',
                                         lead_time_demand + safety_stock)
        
        # Trend momentum indicator
        # Rate of change in adoption
        momentum = pm.Deterministic('trend_momentum',
                                    adoption_rate / (cumulative_adoption + 0.001))
        
        # Fashion forward index
        # Combines early adoption, high virality, premium pricing
        fashion_forward_index = pm.Deterministic('fashion_forward_score',
                                                 p * virality * (price / 100))
        
        # Markdown timing
        # When momentum drops below threshold
        markdown_threshold = 0.1
        weeks_to_markdown = pm.Deterministic('weeks_to_markdown',
                                             time_to_peak[trend_type] + 
                                             pm.math.log(markdown_threshold) / 
                                             (-decay_rate[trend_type]))
        
        # Customer lifetime value for trend
        repeat_purchase_rate = pm.Beta('repeat_rate_by_trend',
                                       alpha=[2, 3, 5],  # Higher for classic
                                       beta=[8, 7, 5],
                                       shape=n_types)
        
        avg_purchases = 1 / (1 - repeat_purchase_rate[trend_type])
        
        clv = pm.Deterministic('customer_lifetime_value',
                               avg_purchases * profit)
        
    return model
```

prompt: An NBA analytics team models player injury risk. Data from 450 players: Guards: 180 players, 2.3±1.8 injuries/season. Forwards: 150 players, 2.8±2.1 injuries/season. Centers: 120 players, 3.5±2.4 injuries/season. Age effect: <25yrs: 1.8±1.2, 25-30yrs: 2.5±1.9, >30yrs: 3.9±2.5. Minutes played correlates 0.65 with injury rate. Prior injuries strongest predictor. Model injury risk and optimize rotation.
completion:
<think>
Player injury modeling needs:
1. Hierarchical structure by position
2. Age effects (likely non-linear)
3. Exposure (minutes) adjustment
4. Prior injury history
5. Negative binomial for overdispersed counts

Key is balancing playing time vs injury risk.
</think>

I'll model NBA player injury risk with position and age effects to optimize rotations.

```python
import pymc as pm
import numpy as np

def model(data):
    with pm.Model() as model:
        # Player data
        position = data['position']  # 0=Guard, 1=Forward, 2=Center
        age = data['age']
        minutes_per_game = data['minutes_per_game']
        games_played = data['games_played']
        prior_injuries = data['prior_injuries']
        injury_count = data['injuries_this_season']
        
        n_positions = 3
        
        # Position-specific baseline injury rates
        position_rates = pm.Gamma('position_injury_rate',
                                  alpha=[2.3**2/1.8**2, 2.8**2/2.1**2, 3.5**2/2.4**2],
                                  beta=[2.3/1.8**2, 2.8/2.1**2, 3.5/2.4**2],
                                  shape=n_positions)
        
        # Age effect with spline (non-linear)
        age_knots = np.array([22, 25, 28, 31, 35])
        n_knots = len(age_knots)
        
        # B-spline basis
        def bspline_basis(x, knots, degree=3):
            n = len(knots) + degree + 1
            augmented_knots = np.concatenate([np.repeat(knots[0], degree), knots, np.repeat(knots[-1], degree)])
            basis = np.zeros((len(x), n-degree-1))
            for i in range(n-degree-1):
                basis[:, i] = pm.math.switch((x >= augmented_knots[i]) & (x < augmented_knots[i+degree+1]), 1.0, 0.0)
            return basis
        
        age_basis = bspline_basis(age, age_knots)
        age_coeffs = pm.Normal('age_spline_coeffs', mu=0, sigma=1, shape=age_basis.shape[1])
        age_effect = pm.math.exp(pm.math.dot(age_basis, age_coeffs))
        
        # Minutes exposure effect
        minutes_coefficient = pm.Normal('minutes_coefficient', mu=0.65, sigma=0.1)
        minutes_effect = (minutes_per_game / 20) ** minutes_coefficient  # Normalized to 20 min/game
        
        # Prior injury effect (injury begets injury)
        prior_injury_multiplier = pm.Normal('prior_injury_effect', mu=0.3, sigma=0.05)
        injury_history_effect = pm.math.exp(prior_injury_multiplier * prior_injuries)
        
        # Games played exposure
        exposure = games_played / 82  # Full season = 82 games
        
        # Individual player effects (random effects)
        player_sigma = pm.HalfNormal('player_sigma', sigma=0.5)
        n_players = len(position)
        player_effects = pm.Normal('player_effects', mu=0, sigma=player_sigma, shape=n_players)
        
        # Expected injuries
        expected_injuries = (position_rates[position] * 
                           age_effect * 
                           minutes_effect * 
                           injury_history_effect * 
                           pm.math.exp(player_effects) * 
                           exposure)
        
        # Overdispersion parameter
        alpha = pm.Exponential('overdispersion', 1.0)
        
        # Observation model
        injuries_obs = pm.NegativeBinomial('injuries_observed',
                                          mu=expected_injuries,
                                          alpha=alpha,
                                          observed=injury_count)
        
        # Derived quantities for rotation optimization
        # Injury risk per 100 minutes
        risk_per_100min = pm.Deterministic('risk_per_100min',
                                          expected_injuries / (minutes_per_game * games_played / 100))
        
        # Optimal minutes threshold (where marginal risk increases sharply)
        optimal_minutes = pm.Deterministic('optimal_minutes_per_game',
                                          20 * pm.math.exp(-1/minutes_coefficient))
        
        # High risk players (>4 expected injuries)
        high_risk_flag = pm.Deterministic('high_risk_player',
                                         expected_injuries > 4)
        
        # Position-specific age curves
        ages_to_evaluate = np.linspace(19, 38, 20)
        age_curves = []
        for pos in range(n_positions):
            age_basis_eval = bspline_basis(ages_to_evaluate, age_knots)
            age_curve = position_rates[pos] * pm.math.exp(pm.math.dot(age_basis_eval, age_coeffs))
            age_curves.append(age_curve)
        
        peak_age_guard = pm.Deterministic('peak_age_guards',
                                         ages_to_evaluate[pm.math.argmin(age_curves[0])])
        peak_age_forward = pm.Deterministic('peak_age_forwards',
                                           ages_to_evaluate[pm.math.argmin(age_curves[1])])
        peak_age_center = pm.Deterministic('peak_age_centers',
                                          ages_to_evaluate[pm.math.argmin(age_curves[2])])
        
        # Load management recommendations
        # Back-to-back game risk multiplier
        b2b_multiplier = pm.Beta('back_to_back_risk_multiplier', alpha=3, beta=1)
        
        # Rest games needed to reduce risk by 50%
        rest_effect = pm.Beta('rest_recovery_rate', alpha=2, beta=2)
        games_rest_for_50pct_reduction = pm.Deterministic('rest_games_needed',
                                                         -pm.math.log(0.5) / pm.math.log(1 - rest_effect))
        
        # Career longevity prediction
        # Survival model for career length
        career_hazard_base = pm.Gamma('career_hazard_base', alpha=2, beta=20)
        injury_hazard_multiplier = pm.Normal('injury_career_impact', mu=1.2, sigma=0.2)
        
        career_hazard = career_hazard_base * injury_hazard_multiplier ** injury_count
        expected_remaining_years = pm.Deterministic('expected_career_years_remaining',
                                                   1 / career_hazard)
        
        # Team-level metrics
        # Total team injury burden
        team_injury_load = pm.Deterministic('team_total_injury_risk',
                                           pm.math.sum(expected_injuries))
        
        # Rotation depth needed
        roster_cushion = pm.Deterministic('extra_players_needed',
                                         team_injury_load / 5)  # Assuming 5 starters
        
        # Financial impact
        salary_per_player = data.get('salary', np.ones_like(position) * 10)  # Default $10M
        injury_cost = pm.Deterministic('injury_cost_millions',
                                      pm.math.sum(expected_injuries * salary_per_player / games_played))
        
        return model
```

prompt: A city's transit authority optimizes bus routes using ridership data. 15 routes analyzed: Downtown routes: 8500±1200 daily riders, 18 min headway. Suburban: 3200±800 riders, 35 min headway. Express: 5500±1000 riders, 25 min headway. Weather effects: Rain -15%, Snow -35%, Heat >90F -10%. Major events +40% on affected routes. Peak hours: 7-9am, 5-7pm show 3x base ridership. Model demand and optimize schedule.
completion:
<think>
Transit optimization requires:
1. Route-type hierarchical model
2. Temporal patterns (hourly, daily, seasonal)
3. Weather and event effects
4. Capacity constraints
5. Service level optimization

Need to balance frequency with demand while minimizing wait times.
</think>

Let me create a count-based model that handles discrete counts.

```python
import pymc as pm
import numpy as np

def model(data):
    with pm.Model() as model:
        # Route data
        route_type = data['route_type']  # 0=Downtown, 1=Suburban, 2=Express
        hour_of_day = data['hour']
        day_of_week = data['day_of_week']  # 0=Monday, 6=Sunday
        weather = data['weather']  # 0=Clear, 1=Rain, 2=Snow, 3=Heat
        has_event = data['has_major_event']
        current_headway = data['headway_minutes']
        ridership = data['ridership']
        
        n_route_types = 3
        n_hours = 24
        n_weather = 4
        
        # Base ridership by route type
        base_ridership = pm.Gamma('base_ridership',
                                  alpha=[8500**2/1200**2, 3200**2/800**2, 5500**2/1000**2],
                                  beta=[8500/1200**2, 3200/800**2, 5500/1000**2],
                                  shape=n_route_types)
        
        # Hourly patterns
        # Peak hours multiplier
        is_morning_peak = pm.math.switch((hour_of_day >= 7) & (hour_of_day < 9), 1, 0)
        is_evening_peak = pm.math.switch((hour_of_day >= 17) & (hour_of_day < 19), 1, 0)
        is_peak = is_morning_peak + is_evening_peak
        
        peak_multiplier = pm.Normal('peak_multiplier', mu=3.0, sigma=0.3)
        off_peak_factor = pm.Beta('off_peak_factor', alpha=2, beta=3)  # ~0.4
        
        # Smooth hourly curve using Fourier series
        n_harmonics = 3
        hourly_coeffs_cos = pm.Normal('hourly_cos_coeffs', mu=0, sigma=0.5, shape=n_harmonics)
        hourly_coeffs_sin = pm.Normal('hourly_sin_coeffs', mu=0, sigma=0.5, shape=n_harmonics)
        
        hourly_pattern = 1.0
        for k in range(n_harmonics):
            hourly_pattern += (hourly_coeffs_cos[k] * pm.math.cos(2 * np.pi * (k+1) * hour_of_day / 24) +
                              hourly_coeffs_sin[k] * pm.math.sin(2 * np.pi * (k+1) * hour_of_day / 24))
        
        # Combine peak and smooth patterns
        time_effect = pm.math.switch(is_peak, peak_multiplier, off_peak_factor * hourly_pattern)
        
        # Day of week effects
        weekday_factors = pm.Dirichlet('weekday_factors', a=np.ones(7) * 5)
        day_effect = weekday_factors[day_of_week] * 7  # Normalize to average 1
        
        # Weather effects
        weather_effects = pm.Normal('weather_effects',
                                   mu=[0, -0.15, -0.35, -0.10],  # Clear, Rain, Snow, Heat
                                   sigma=0.05,
                                   shape=n_weather)
        weather_multiplier = pm.math.exp(weather_effects[weather])
        
        # Event effect
        event_multiplier = pm.Normal('event_multiplier', mu=0.4, sigma=0.1)
        event_effect = pm.math.switch(has_event, 1 + event_multiplier, 1.0)
        
        # Route-specific random effects
        route_sigma = pm.HalfNormal('route_sigma', sigma=0.2)
        n_routes = len(route_type)
        route_effects = pm.Normal('route_effects', mu=0, sigma=route_sigma, shape=n_routes)
        
        # Expected ridership
        expected_ridership = (base_ridership[route_type] * 
                            time_effect * 
                            day_effect * 
                            weather_multiplier * 
                            event_effect * 
                            pm.math.exp(route_effects))
        
        # Observation model (Negative Binomial)
        alpha = pm.Exponential('overdispersion', 0.1)
        ridership_obs = pm.NegativeBinomial('ridership_observed',
                                           mu=expected_ridership,
                                           alpha=alpha,
                                           observed=ridership)
        
        # Service optimization
        # Capacity per bus
        bus_capacity = 60  # seats + standing
        load_factor_target = 0.85  # Don't pack buses completely
        
        # Required buses per hour
        buses_needed = pm.Deterministic('buses_per_hour_needed',
                                       expected_ridership / (bus_capacity * load_factor_target))
        
        # Optimal headway (minutes)
        optimal_headway = pm.Deterministic('optimal_headway_minutes',
                                          60 / buses_needed)
        
        # Waiting time model (average wait = headway/2)
        current_wait = current_headway / 2
        optimal_wait = optimal_headway / 2
        wait_time_reduction = pm.Deterministic('wait_time_reduction_minutes',
                                              current_wait - optimal_wait)
        
        # Service reliability
        # Poisson arrival assumption
        headway_variance = pm.HalfNormal('headway_variance_minutes', sigma=5)
        reliability = pm.Deterministic('on_time_performance',
                                      pm.math.exp(-headway_variance**2 / (2 * current_headway**2)))
        
        # Crowding discomfort (exponential increase over 100% capacity)
        load_factor = expected_ridership / (60 / current_headway * bus_capacity)
        crowding_penalty = pm.Deterministic('crowding_discomfort_index',
                                           pm.math.switch(load_factor > 1.0,
                                                         pm.math.exp(2 * (load_factor - 1)),
                                                         1.0))
        
        # Cost optimization
        cost_per_bus_hour = 150  # Operating cost
        current_cost = 60 / current_headway * cost_per_bus_hour
        optimal_cost = buses_needed * cost_per_bus_hour
        
        cost_per_rider = pm.Deterministic('cost_per_rider',
                                         optimal_cost / expected_ridership)
        
        # Fleet requirements
        # Account for peak spreading and deadheading
        peak_spread_factor = 1.2
        deadhead_factor = 1.15
        fleet_size_needed = pm.Deterministic('fleet_size_required',
                                            pm.math.max(buses_needed) * peak_spread_factor * deadhead_factor)
        
        # Environmental impact
        emissions_per_bus_mile = 2.5  # kg CO2
        miles_per_hour = 12  # Average speed
        emissions_saved_vs_cars = pm.Deterministic('emissions_saved_kg_co2',
                                                  expected_ridership * 0.5 - buses_needed * miles_per_hour * emissions_per_bus_mile)
        
        # Service span optimization
        # When does ridership drop below sustainable threshold?
        min_sustainable_ridership = 10  # riders per bus
        service_hours = pm.Deterministic('recommended_service_hours',
                                        pm.math.sum(pm.math.switch(expected_ridership > min_sustainable_ridership * buses_needed,
                                                                   1, 0)))
        
        # Route performance score
        # Combines ridership, cost efficiency, and service quality
        performance_score = pm.Deterministic('route_performance_score',
                                            expected_ridership / optimal_cost * reliability * (2 - crowding_penalty))
        
        return model
```

prompt: A school district analyzes student performance to identify at-risk learners. 5000 students: Elementary (K-5): 2000 students, 15% at-risk. Middle (6-8): 1500 students, 22% at-risk. High (9-12): 1500 students, 28% at-risk. Risk factors: Attendance <90%: OR=3.2, Failed previous year: OR=4.5, Low engagement score: OR=2.8, Free lunch: OR=1.9. Early intervention reduces risk by 45%. Model risk and intervention effectiveness.
completion:
<think>
Student at-risk modeling needs:
1. Hierarchical structure by school level
2. Multiple risk factors with odds ratios
3. Interaction effects between factors
4. Intervention effectiveness modeling
5. Early warning system design

Logistic regression with careful handling of correlations.
</think>

To model this, I'll employ logit link function for binary outcomes.

```python
import pymc as pm
import numpy as np

def model(data):
    with pm.Model() as model:
        # Student data
        school_level = data['school_level']  # 0=Elementary, 1=Middle, 2=High
        attendance_rate = data['attendance_rate']
        failed_previous = data['failed_previous_year']
        engagement_score = data['engagement_score']  # 0-100
        free_lunch = data['receives_free_lunch']
        received_intervention = data['received_intervention']
        is_at_risk = data['is_at_risk']
        
        n_levels = 3
        
        # Base risk by school level
        base_risk_logit = pm.Normal('base_risk_logit',
                                   mu=pm.math.log([0.15/0.85, 0.22/0.78, 0.28/0.72]),
                                   sigma=0.3,
                                   shape=n_levels)
        
        # Risk factor effects (log odds ratios)
        attendance_or = pm.Normal('attendance_odds_ratio', mu=pm.math.log(3.2), sigma=0.2)
        failed_or = pm.Normal('failed_year_odds_ratio', mu=pm.math.log(4.5), sigma=0.2)
        engagement_or = pm.Normal('low_engagement_odds_ratio', mu=pm.math.log(2.8), sigma=0.2)
        free_lunch_or = pm.Normal('free_lunch_odds_ratio', mu=pm.math.log(1.9), sigma=0.2)
        
        # Attendance effect (continuous)
        low_attendance = pm.math.switch(attendance_rate < 0.9, 1, 0)
        attendance_effect = attendance_or * low_attendance
        
        # Engagement effect (continuous with threshold)
        low_engagement = pm.math.switch(engagement_score < 50, 1, 0)
        engagement_continuous = pm.Normal('engagement_slope', mu=-0.02, sigma=0.005)
        engagement_effect = engagement_or * low_engagement + engagement_continuous * (50 - engagement_score)
        
        # School-level random effects
        school_sigma = pm.HalfNormal('school_sigma', sigma=0.5)
        n_schools = 20  # Assume 20 schools in district
        school_effects = pm.Normal('school_effects', mu=0, sigma=school_sigma, shape=n_schools)
        school_id = data.get('school_id', np.random.randint(0, n_schools, len(school_level)))
        
        # Teacher/classroom effects (nested)
        teacher_sigma = pm.HalfNormal('teacher_sigma', sigma=0.3)
        n_teachers = 200
        teacher_effects = pm.Normal('teacher_effects', mu=0, sigma=teacher_sigma, shape=n_teachers)
        teacher_id = data.get('teacher_id', np.random.randint(0, n_teachers, len(school_level)))
        
        # Interaction effects
        # Failed + Low attendance is particularly bad
        failed_attendance_interaction = pm.Normal('failed_attendance_interaction', mu=0.5, sigma=0.2)
        interaction_effect = failed_attendance_interaction * failed_previous * low_attendance
        
        # Total risk (logit scale)
        logit_risk = (base_risk_logit[school_level] +
                     attendance_effect +
                     failed_or * failed_previous +
                     engagement_effect +
                     free_lunch_or * free_lunch +
                     interaction_effect +
                     school_effects[school_id] +
                     teacher_effects[teacher_id])
        
        # Intervention effect
        intervention_effectiveness = pm.Beta('intervention_effectiveness', alpha=4.5, beta=5.5)  # ~0.45
        intervention_effect = pm.math.switch(received_intervention,
                                           -pm.math.log(1/(1-intervention_effectiveness) - 1),
                                           0)
        
        # Final risk probability
        risk_probability = pm.Deterministic('risk_probability',
                                           pm.math.sigmoid(logit_risk + intervention_effect))
        
        # Observation model
        at_risk_obs = pm.Bernoulli('at_risk_observed',
                                   p=risk_probability,
                                   observed=is_at_risk)
        
        # Early warning system
        # Risk score for screening
        risk_score = pm.Deterministic('risk_screening_score',
                                     pm.math.sigmoid(logit_risk) * 100)
        
        # Thresholds for intervention
        high_risk_threshold = pm.Deterministic('high_risk_threshold',
                                              pm.math.invlogit(base_risk_logit[school_level] + pm.math.log(2)))
        
        very_high_risk_threshold = pm.Deterministic('very_high_risk_threshold',
                                                   pm.math.invlogit(base_risk_logit[school_level] + pm.math.log(4)))
        
        # Number needed to treat
        baseline_risk = pm.math.sigmoid(base_risk_logit[school_level])
        risk_reduction = baseline_risk * intervention_effectiveness
        nnt = pm.Deterministic('number_needed_to_treat',
                              1 / risk_reduction)
        
        # Cost-benefit analysis
        intervention_cost = 2000  # $ per student per year
        at_risk_cost = 15000  # Average additional cost of at-risk student
        
        cost_per_success = pm.Deterministic('intervention_cost_per_success',
                                           intervention_cost * nnt)
        
        benefit_cost_ratio = pm.Deterministic('benefit_cost_ratio',
                                             at_risk_cost / cost_per_success)
        
        # Predictive metrics by grade level
        # Transition risk (elementary to middle, middle to high)
        grade_transition_multiplier = pm.Normal('grade_transition_risk_multiplier',
                                               mu=[1.5, 1.3],  # Elementary->Middle, Middle->High
                                               sigma=0.2,
                                               shape=2)
        
        # Long-term outcomes
        # Graduation probability based on risk history
        n_years_at_risk = data.get('years_at_risk', np.zeros_like(is_at_risk))
        graduation_base = pm.Beta('graduation_rate_base', alpha=85, beta=15)  # ~85%
        
        risk_impact_on_graduation = pm.Normal('risk_year_graduation_impact', mu=-0.15, sigma=0.03)
        graduation_probability = pm.Deterministic('graduation_probability',
                                                 graduation_base * pm.math.exp(risk_impact_on_graduation * n_years_at_risk))
        
        # Resource allocation
        # Optimal intervention capacity
        total_at_risk = pm.math.sum(risk_probability)
        intervention_capacity_needed = pm.Deterministic('intervention_capacity_needed',
                                                       total_at_risk * 1.2)  # 20% buffer
        
        # Priority score for intervention
        # Combines risk level, potential benefit, and cost
        priority_score = pm.Deterministic('intervention_priority_score',
                                         risk_probability * (1 - graduation_probability) * at_risk_cost)
        
        # School performance metrics
        school_risk_rate = pm.Deterministic('school_average_risk_rate',
                                           pm.math.sum(risk_probability) / len(risk_probability))
        
        # Early indicator validation
        # How well do 3rd grade indicators predict 8th grade outcomes?
        early_indicator_reliability = pm.Beta('early_indicator_reliability', alpha=7, beta=3)  # ~0.7
        
        return model
```

prompt: A wind farm operator forecasts power generation. 50 turbines: 2.5MW rated capacity each. Wind speed: Cut-in 3m/s, rated 12m/s, cut-out 25m/s. Current: 8.5±2.1m/s. Power curve non-linear: cubic below rated. Capacity factors: Spring 35%, Summer 25%, Fall 30%, Winter 40%. Turbulence intensity affects efficiency -2% per 0.1 increase. Maintenance downtime 3%. Model generation and optimize maintenance schedule.
completion:
<think>
Wind power modeling requires:
1. Non-linear power curves
2. Weibull wind distribution
3. Seasonal patterns
4. Turbulence effects
5. Maintenance optimization

Key is the cubic relationship between wind speed and power below rated speed.
</think>

Here's how we can approach this with censoring.

```python
import pymc as pm
import numpy as np

def model(data):
    with pm.Model() as model:
        # Wind farm data
        wind_speed = data['wind_speed']  # m/s
        turbulence_intensity = data['turbulence_intensity']  # 0-1
        season = data['season']  # 0=Spring, 1=Summer, 2=Fall, 3=Winter
        turbine_age = data['turbine_age']  # years
        is_maintained = data['recently_maintained']  # within last month
        power_output = data['power_output_mw']
        
        n_turbines = 50
        rated_capacity = 2.5  # MW per turbine
        total_capacity = n_turbines * rated_capacity
        
        # Wind speed parameters (Weibull distribution)
        # Shape parameter k
        weibull_k = pm.Normal('weibull_shape', mu=2.0, sigma=0.3)
        
        # Scale parameter by season
        seasonal_scales = pm.Gamma('seasonal_wind_scale',
                                  alpha=[8.5**2/2.1**2] * 4,
                                  beta=[8.5/2.1**2] * 4,
                                  shape=4)
        
        # Power curve parameters
        cut_in_speed = 3.0
        rated_speed = 12.0
        cut_out_speed = 25.0
        
        # Capacity factors by season
        capacity_factors = pm.Beta('seasonal_capacity_factors',
                                  alpha=[35*2, 25*3, 30*2.5, 40*2],
                                  beta=[65*2, 75*3, 70*2.5, 60*2],
                                  shape=4)
        
        # Turbulence effect
        turbulence_coefficient = pm.Normal('turbulence_impact', mu=-0.2, sigma=0.05)  # -2% per 0.1
        turbulence_effect = 1 + turbulence_coefficient * turbulence_intensity
        
        # Age degradation
        annual_degradation = pm.Beta('annual_degradation_rate', alpha=2, beta=200)  # ~1% per year
        age_effect = (1 - annual_degradation) ** turbine_age
        
        # Maintenance effect
        maintenance_boost = pm.Normal('maintenance_effectiveness', mu=0.03, sigma=0.01)
        maintenance_effect = pm.math.switch(is_maintained, 1 + maintenance_boost, 1.0)
        
        # Non-linear power curve
        def power_curve(v):
            # Below cut-in: no power
            below_cut_in = pm.math.switch(v < cut_in_speed, 0, 1)
            
            # Between cut-in and rated: cubic relationship
            # P = rated_power * ((v^3 - v_cut_in^3) / (v_rated^3 - v_cut_in^3))
            cubic_region = pm.math.switch((v >= cut_in_speed) & (v < rated_speed),
                                         rated_capacity * ((v**3 - cut_in_speed**3) / 
                                                          (rated_speed**3 - cut_in_speed**3)),
                                         0)
            
            # At and above rated: constant until cut-out
            rated_region = pm.math.switch((v >= rated_speed) & (v <= cut_out_speed),
                                         rated_capacity,
                                         0)
            
            # Above cut-out: no power (safety shutdown)
            power = below_cut_in * (cubic_region + rated_region)
            
            return power
        
        # Expected power per turbine
        theoretical_power = power_curve(wind_speed)
        
        # Apply all effects
        expected_power_per_turbine = (theoretical_power * 
                                     capacity_factors[season] * 
                                     turbulence_effect * 
                                     age_effect * 
                                     maintenance_effect)
        
        # Availability (1 - downtime)
        base_availability = pm.Beta('base_availability', alpha=97, beta=3)  # ~97%
        
        # Number of operational turbines
        operational_turbines = pm.Binomial('operational_turbines',
                                          n=n_turbines,
                                          p=base_availability)
        
        # Total farm output
        farm_output = operational_turbines * expected_power_per_turbine
        
        # Observation noise
        output_sigma = pm.HalfNormal('output_measurement_error', sigma=2)
        
        # Observed power
        power_obs = pm.Normal('power_observed',
                             mu=farm_output,
                             sigma=output_sigma,
                             observed=power_output)
        
        # Optimization metrics
        # Capacity factor
        current_capacity_factor = pm.Deterministic('current_capacity_factor',
                                                  farm_output / total_capacity)
        
        # Annual energy production (AEP)
        hours_per_year = 8760
        aep_gwh = pm.Deterministic('annual_energy_production_gwh',
                                  farm_output * hours_per_year / 1000)
        
        # Optimal maintenance timing
        # Maintenance benefit vs lost production
        maintenance_downtime_hours = 48  # 2 days
        maintenance_lost_revenue = expected_power_per_turbine * maintenance_downtime_hours * 0.05  # $/MWh
        
        maintenance_benefit_hours = 30 * 24  # Benefit lasts ~1 month
        maintenance_added_revenue = (expected_power_per_turbine * maintenance_boost * 
                                   maintenance_benefit_hours * 0.05)
        
        maintenance_roi = pm.Deterministic('maintenance_roi',
                                         maintenance_added_revenue / maintenance_lost_revenue)
        
        # Optimal wind speed for maintenance (lowest production)
        optimal_maintenance_wind = pm.Deterministic('optimal_maintenance_wind_speed',
                                                  (cut_in_speed + rated_speed) / 2)
        
        # Wake loss estimation
        # Turbines affect downstream turbines
        wake_loss_factor = pm.Beta('wake_loss_factor', alpha=9, beta=1)  # ~10% loss
        effective_turbines = pm.Deterministic('effective_turbines_after_wake',
                                            operational_turbines * wake_loss_factor)
        
        # Economic metrics
        electricity_price = 50  # $/MWh
        revenue_per_hour = pm.Deterministic('revenue_per_hour',
                                          farm_output * electricity_price)
        
        # Payback calculation
        turbine_cost = 2.5e6  # $ per turbine
        total_investment = n_turbines * turbine_cost
        annual_revenue = revenue_per_hour * hours_per_year
        simple_payback_years = pm.Deterministic('simple_payback_years',
                                              total_investment / annual_revenue)
        
        # Reliability metrics
        # Weibull reliability model for turbines
        failure_shape = pm.Normal('failure_weibull_shape', mu=2.5, sigma=0.5)
        failure_scale = pm.Normal('failure_weibull_scale_years', mu=20, sigma=3)
        
        reliability = pm.Deterministic('turbine_reliability',
                                     pm.math.exp(-(turbine_age / failure_scale) ** failure_shape))
        
        mtbf_years = pm.Deterministic('mean_time_between_failures_years',
                                    failure_scale * pm.math.exp(pm.math.lgamma(1 + 1/failure_shape)))
        
        # Grid integration
        # Ramp rate constraints
        max_ramp_rate = 0.1 * total_capacity  # 10% per minute
        wind_volatility = pm.HalfNormal('wind_speed_volatility', sigma=0.5)
        
        expected_ramps = pm.Deterministic('expected_ramp_events_per_day',
                                        24 * wind_volatility / max_ramp_rate)
        
        # Carbon offset
        carbon_intensity_grid = 0.5  # tons CO2/MWh
        carbon_offset_tons = pm.Deterministic('annual_carbon_offset_tons',
                                            aep_gwh * 1000 * carbon_intensity_grid)
        
        # Future performance projection
        years_ahead = 10
        future_degradation = (1 - annual_degradation) ** years_ahead
        future_capacity_factor = pm.Deterministic('capacity_factor_in_10_years',
                                                current_capacity_factor * future_degradation)
        
        return model
```

prompt: A hotel chain optimizes pricing and occupancy. 200-room property: Weekday occupancy 65±12%, weekend 85±8%. Base rate $150. Price elasticity -1.8. Seasonal: Summer +20%, Winter -15%. Events within 5 miles increase demand 30%. Competitor rates affect: +$10 → -5% occupancy. Cancellation rate 12%. Model revenue optimization with dynamic pricing.
completion:
<think>
Hotel revenue management needs:
1. Demand modeling with multiple factors
2. Price elasticity and competitor effects
3. Seasonal and event impacts
4. Cancellation/overbooking strategy
5. Dynamic pricing optimization

RevPAR (Revenue per Available Room) is key metric.
</think>

I'll model hotel occupancy and implement dynamic pricing optimization.

```python
import pymc as pm
import numpy as np

def model(data):
    with pm.Model() as model:
        # Hotel data
        is_weekend = data['is_weekend']
        season = data['season']  # 0=Spring, 1=Summer, 2=Fall, 3=Winter
        has_event = data['has_nearby_event']
        competitor_rate_diff = data['competitor_rate_difference']  # Our rate - competitor rate
        days_until_arrival = data['days_until_arrival']
        current_price = data['room_rate']
        occupancy = data['occupancy_rate']
        
        n_rooms = 200
        base_rate = 150
        
        # Base occupancy rates
        weekday_occupancy = pm.Beta('weekday_occupancy_rate',
                                   alpha=65**2/(12**2),
                                   beta=(35*65)/(12**2))
        
        weekend_occupancy = pm.Beta('weekend_occupancy_rate',
                                   alpha=85**2/(8**2),
                                   beta=(15*85)/(8**2))
        
        base_occupancy = pm.math.switch(is_weekend, weekend_occupancy, weekday_occupancy)
        
        # Price elasticity
        price_elasticity = pm.Normal('price_elasticity', mu=-1.8, sigma=0.2)
        price_ratio = current_price / base_rate
        price_effect = price_ratio ** price_elasticity
        
        # Seasonal effects
        seasonal_effects = pm.Normal('seasonal_demand_effects',
                                    mu=[0, 0.20, 0, -0.15],  # Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter
                                    sigma=0.05,
                                    shape=4)
        season_multiplier = 1 + seasonal_effects[season]
        
        # Event effect
        event_lift = pm.Normal('event_demand_lift', mu=0.30, sigma=0.05)
        event_effect = pm.math.switch(has_event, 1 + event_lift, 1.0)
        
        # Competitor effect
        competitor_sensitivity = pm.Normal('competitor_price_sensitivity', mu=-0.005, sigma=0.001)  # -0.5% per $
        competitor_effect = pm.math.exp(competitor_sensitivity * competitor_rate_diff)
        
        # Booking curve (closer to arrival = higher demand)
        booking_curve_param = pm.Normal('booking_curve_steepness', mu=0.02, sigma=0.005)
        booking_urgency = pm.math.exp(-booking_curve_param * days_until_arrival)
        
        # Day of week patterns
        dow_effects = pm.Dirichlet('day_of_week_effects', a=np.ones(7))
        dow = data.get('day_of_week', np.zeros_like(is_weekend, dtype=int))
        dow_multiplier = dow_effects[dow] * 7  # Normalize
        
        # Expected demand (can exceed 1.0)
        expected_demand = (base_occupancy * 
                         price_effect * 
                         season_multiplier * 
                         event_effect * 
                         competitor_effect * 
                         booking_urgency * 
                         dow_multiplier)
        
        # Cancellation rate
        base_cancellation_rate = pm.Beta('base_cancellation_rate',
                                        alpha=12*8, beta=88*8)  # ~12%
        
        # Cancellations increase with advance booking
        advance_cancellation_factor = pm.Normal('advance_booking_cancellation_factor',
                                              mu=0.001, sigma=0.0002)
        cancellation_rate = base_cancellation_rate * (1 + advance_cancellation_factor * days_until_arrival)
        
        # Net demand after cancellations
        net_demand = expected_demand * (1 - cancellation_rate)
        
        # Constrain to [0, 1] for occupancy
        occupancy_rate = pm.math.minimum(net_demand, 1.0)
        
        # Observation model
        occupancy_obs = pm.Beta('occupancy_observed',
                               alpha=occupancy_rate * 100,
                               beta=(1 - occupancy_rate) * 100,
                               observed=occupancy)
        
        # Revenue optimization
        # Revenue per available room (RevPAR)
        revpar = pm.Deterministic('revpar',
                                 occupancy_rate * current_price)
        
        # Optimal price calculation
        # Derivative of revenue function
        # R = P * D(P) where D(P) = base_demand * (P/base)^elasticity
        # dR/dP = 0 => P_opt = base_price * (-elasticity / (elasticity + 1))
        
        optimal_price_ratio = -price_elasticity / (price_elasticity + 1)
        base_demand = (base_occupancy * season_multiplier * event_effect * 
                      competitor_effect * booking_urgency)
        
        optimal_price = pm.Deterministic('optimal_room_rate',
                                       base_rate * optimal_price_ratio * 
                                       pm.math.minimum(1/base_demand, 2.0))  # Cap at 2x for reasonableness
        
        # Expected revenue at optimal price
        optimal_occupancy = pm.math.minimum(base_demand * optimal_price_ratio ** price_elasticity, 1.0)
        optimal_revpar = pm.Deterministic('optimal_revpar',
                                        optimal_occupancy * optimal_price)
        
        revenue_improvement = pm.Deterministic('revenue_improvement_percent',
                                             (optimal_revpar - revpar) / revpar * 100)
        
        # Overbooking strategy
        # How many rooms to overbook given cancellation rate?
        safety_factor = 0.95  # Don't overbook too aggressively
        overbook_rooms = pm.Deterministic('recommended_overbook_rooms',
                                        n_rooms * cancellation_rate * safety_factor)
        
        # Walk cost (when overbooked guests must be relocated)
        walk_cost = 300  # Cost to walk a guest
        
        # Probability of walking guests
        actual_arrivals = pm.Binomial('actual_arrivals',
                                     n=pm.math.round(n_rooms + overbook_rooms),
                                     p=1 - cancellation_rate)
        
        walks_needed = pm.math.maximum(actual_arrivals - n_rooms, 0)
        walk_probability = pm.Deterministic('walk_probability',
                                          walks_needed / n_rooms)
        
        # Length of stay distribution
        avg_los = pm.Gamma('average_length_of_stay', alpha=4, beta=2)  # ~2 nights
        
        # Total revenue per guest
        revenue_per_guest = current_price * avg_los
        
        # Ancillary revenue (F&B, spa, etc.)
        ancillary_per_night = pm.Gamma('ancillary_revenue_per_night',
                                      alpha=50**2/30**2,
                                      beta=50/30**2)  # ~$50/night
        
        total_revenue_per_guest = pm.Deterministic('total_revenue_per_guest',
                                                  revenue_per_guest + ancillary_per_night * avg_los)
        
        # Market segmentation
        # Business vs leisure mix
        business_fraction = pm.Beta('business_traveler_fraction',
                                   alpha=4, beta=6)  # ~40% business
        
        # Business travelers less price sensitive
        business_elasticity_ratio = pm.Normal('business_elasticity_ratio',
                                            mu=0.5, sigma=0.1)  # Half as elastic
        
        segment_weighted_elasticity = (business_fraction * price_elasticity * business_elasticity_ratio +
                                     (1 - business_fraction) * price_elasticity)
        
        # Loyalty program impact
        loyalty_fraction = pm.Beta('loyalty_member_fraction',
                                  alpha=3, beta=7)  # ~30%
        loyalty_rate_premium = pm.Normal('loyalty_willingness_to_pay_premium',
                                        mu=1.1, sigma=0.05)  # 10% premium
        
        # Competition intensity
        market_concentration = pm.Beta('market_hhi', alpha=2, beta=8)  # Herfindahl index
        pricing_power = pm.Deterministic('hotel_pricing_power',
                                       market_concentration ** 0.5)
        
        # Forecast accuracy by horizon
        forecast_error_rate = pm.Normal('forecast_error_per_day', mu=0.01, sigma=0.002)
        forecast_accuracy = pm.Deterministic('forecast_accuracy',
                                           pm.math.exp(-forecast_error_rate * days_until_arrival))
        
        # Channel distribution
        # Direct bookings more profitable
        direct_booking_rate = pm.Beta('direct_booking_rate',
                                     alpha=2, beta=3)  # ~40% direct
        ota_commission = 0.15  # 15% commission
        
        effective_revpar = pm.Deterministic('effective_revpar_after_commission',
                                          revpar * (direct_booking_rate + 
                                                   (1 - direct_booking_rate) * (1 - ota_commission)))
        
        # Group vs transient mix
        group_fraction = pm.Beta('group_booking_fraction',
                                alpha=1, beta=4)  # ~20% group
        group_discount = pm.Normal('group_rate_discount', mu=0.8, sigma=0.05)  # 20% discount
        
        # Minimum length of stay optimization
        # For high demand periods
        high_demand_threshold = 0.9
        should_require_min_los = pm.Deterministic('require_minimum_stay',
                                                occupancy_rate > high_demand_threshold)
        
        optimal_min_los = pm.Deterministic('optimal_minimum_los',
                                         pm.math.switch(should_require_min_los,
                                                       pm.math.ceil(avg_los), 1))
        
        return model
```

prompt: A legal analytics firm models criminal case outcomes. 5000 cases analyzed: Theft: 2000 cases, 65% convicted. Assault: 1500 cases, 72% convicted. Drug: 1000 cases, 78% convicted. Fraud: 500 cases, 55% convicted. Factors: Prior record increases conviction +25%, private attorney -18%, plea bargain -45%. Judge effects ±15%. Demographics and evidence quality impact sentencing. Model outcomes and identify bias.
completion:
<think>
Legal outcome modeling requires:
1. Hierarchical model by case type and judge
2. Defendant characteristics and representation
3. Plea bargaining effects
4. Potential bias detection
5. Sentencing severity modeling

Need careful handling of selection bias and fairness metrics.
</think>

Let me create a Bayesian logistic model that models probability of success.

```python
import pymc as pm
import numpy as np

def model(data):
    with pm.Model() as model:
        # Case data
        case_type = data['case_type']  # 0=Theft, 1=Assault, 2=Drug, 3=Fraud
        has_prior_record = data['has_prior_record']
        has_private_attorney = data['has_private_attorney']
        accepted_plea = data['accepted_plea_bargain']
        judge_id = data['judge_id']
        defendant_age = data['defendant_age']
        defendant_race = data['defendant_race']  # For bias analysis
        evidence_strength = data['evidence_strength']  # 0-10 scale
        convicted = data['convicted']
        sentence_length = data['sentence_months']  # If convicted
        
        n_case_types = 4
        n_judges = 25
        n_races = 4  # Simplified for example
        
        # Base conviction rates by case type
        base_conviction_rate = pm.Beta('base_conviction_rate',
                                      alpha=[65*20, 72*20, 78*20, 55*20],
                                      beta=[35*20, 28*20, 22*20, 45*20],
                                      shape=n_case_types)
        
        # Convert to logit scale for linear modeling
        base_conviction_logit = pm.Deterministic('base_conviction_logit',
                                               pm.math.log(base_conviction_rate / (1 - base_conviction_rate)))
        
        # Effect sizes (on logit scale)
        prior_record_effect = pm.Normal('prior_record_effect', mu=pm.math.log(1.25/0.75), sigma=0.2)
        private_attorney_effect = pm.Normal('private_attorney_effect', mu=pm.math.log(0.82/1.18), sigma=0.2)
        plea_bargain_effect = pm.Normal('plea_bargain_effect', mu=pm.math.log(0.55/1.45), sigma=0.2)
        
        # Judge random effects
        judge_sigma = pm.HalfNormal('judge_effect_sigma', sigma=0.3)
        judge_effects = pm.Normal('judge_effects', mu=0, sigma=judge_sigma, shape=n_judges)
        
        # Judge severity consistency
        judge_consistency = pm.Beta('judge_consistency', alpha=8, beta=2)  # High = consistent
        
        # Evidence quality effect
        evidence_coefficient = pm.Normal('evidence_quality_effect', mu=0.3, sigma=0.05)
        evidence_effect = evidence_coefficient * (evidence_strength - 5) / 5  # Centered and scaled
        
        # Age effect (U-shaped - young and old get more leniency)
        age_linear = pm.Normal('age_linear_effect', mu=-0.02, sigma=0.005)
        age_squared = pm.Normal('age_squared_effect', mu=0.0003, sigma=0.0001)
        age_effect = age_linear * (defendant_age - 35) + age_squared * (defendant_age - 35)**2
        
        # Bias detection - race effects (should be zero in fair system)
        race_effects = pm.Normal('race_conviction_effects', mu=0, sigma=0.5, shape=n_races)
        race_bias_magnitude = pm.Deterministic('race_bias_magnitude',
                                             pm.math.sqrt(pm.math.sum(race_effects**2)))
        
        # Interaction: race and attorney type
        race_attorney_interaction = pm.Normal('race_attorney_interaction', mu=0, sigma=0.2, shape=n_races)
        
        # Total conviction probability (logit scale)
        conviction_logit = (base_conviction_logit[case_type] +
                          prior_record_effect * has_prior_record +
                          private_attorney_effect * has_private_attorney +
                          plea_bargain_effect * accepted_plea +
                          judge_effects[judge_id] +
                          evidence_effect +
                          age_effect +
                          race_effects[defendant_race] +
                          race_attorney_interaction[defendant_race] * has_private_attorney)
        
        conviction_prob = pm.Deterministic('conviction_probability',
                                         pm.math.sigmoid(conviction_logit))
        
        # Conviction observations
        conviction_obs = pm.Bernoulli('conviction_observed',
                                     p=conviction_prob,
                                     observed=convicted)
        
        # Sentencing model (for those convicted)
        # Base sentence by crime type
        base_sentence_months = pm.Gamma('base_sentence_months',
                                       alpha=[12**2/8**2, 24**2/12**2, 18**2/10**2, 15**2/10**2],
                                       beta=[12/8**2, 24/12**2, 18/10**2, 15/10**2],
                                       shape=n_case_types)
        
        # Sentencing factors
        prior_sentence_multiplier = pm.Normal('prior_record_sentence_multiplier', mu=1.5, sigma=0.2)
        plea_sentence_reduction = pm.Beta('plea_bargain_sentence_reduction', alpha=4, beta=6)  # ~40% reduction
        
        # Judge sentencing tendencies
        judge_sentence_sigma = pm.HalfNormal('judge_sentence_effect_sigma', sigma=0.3)
        judge_sentence_effects = pm.Normal('judge_sentence_effects', mu=0, sigma=judge_sentence_sigma, shape=n_judges)
        
        # Race effects on sentencing (bias detection)
        race_sentence_effects = pm.Normal('race_sentence_effects', mu=0, sigma=0.3, shape=n_races)
        race_sentence_bias = pm.Deterministic('race_sentence_bias_magnitude',
                                            pm.math.sqrt(pm.math.sum(race_sentence_effects**2)))
        
        # Expected sentence (for convicted defendants)
        convicted_mask = convicted == 1
        if np.any(convicted_mask):
            expected_sentence = (base_sentence_months[case_type[convicted_mask]] *
                               pm.math.switch(has_prior_record[convicted_mask], prior_sentence_multiplier, 1.0) *
                               pm.math.switch(accepted_plea[convicted_mask], 1 - plea_sentence_reduction, 1.0) *
                               pm.math.exp(judge_sentence_effects[judge_id[convicted_mask]]) *
                               pm.math.exp(race_sentence_effects[defendant_race[convicted_mask]]))
            
            # Sentence observations
            sentence_sigma = pm.HalfNormal('sentence_sigma', sigma=5)
            sentence_obs = pm.TruncatedNormal('sentence_observed',
                                            mu=expected_sentence,
                                            sigma=sentence_sigma,
                                            lower=0,
                                            observed=sentence_length[convicted_mask])
        
        # Fairness metrics
        # Disparate impact ratio (should be close to 1)
        baseline_race = 0  # Reference group
        disparate_impact = pm.Deterministic('disparate_impact_ratio',
                                          pm.math.sigmoid(conviction_logit + race_effects) /
                                          pm.math.sigmoid(conviction_logit + race_effects[baseline_race]))
        
        # Statistical parity difference
        statistical_parity_diff = pm.Deterministic('statistical_parity_difference',
                                                 pm.math.abs(pm.math.mean(conviction_prob[defendant_race == 0]) -
                                                           pm.math.mean(conviction_prob[defendant_race == 1])))
        
        # Calibration by race
        calibration_slope = pm.Normal('calibration_slope_by_race', mu=1, sigma=0.2, shape=n_races)
        
        # Case outcome prediction accuracy
        prediction_accuracy = pm.Deterministic('prediction_accuracy',
                                             pm.math.mean(pm.math.switch(conviction_prob > 0.5,
                                                                       convicted,
                                                                       1 - convicted)))
        
        # Attorney effectiveness
        attorney_effectiveness = pm.Deterministic('private_attorney_effectiveness',
                                               1 - pm.math.exp(private_attorney_effect))
        
        # Plea bargain impact
        plea_conviction_reduction = pm.Deterministic('plea_conviction_reduction_pct',
                                                   (1 - pm.math.exp(plea_bargain_effect)) * 100)
        
        plea_sentence_reduction_pct = pm.Deterministic('plea_sentence_reduction_pct',
                                                      plea_sentence_reduction * 100)
        
        # Judge ranking by severity
        judge_severity_rank = pm.Deterministic('judge_severity_ranking',
                                             pm.math.argsort(judge_effects))
        
        # Evidence threshold for conviction
        # What evidence strength gives 50% conviction probability?
        evidence_threshold = pm.Deterministic('evidence_threshold_for_conviction',
                                           5 - (base_conviction_logit / evidence_coefficient))
        
        # Wrongful conviction risk (low evidence but convicted)
        low_evidence_mask = evidence_strength < 3
        wrongful_conviction_rate = pm.Deterministic('potential_wrongful_conviction_rate',
                                                  pm.math.mean(conviction_prob[low_evidence_mask]))
        
        # Selection bias - who accepts plea bargains?
        # Model probability of accepting plea
        plea_accept_logit = (0.5 + 0.3 * evidence_strength/10 + 
                           0.2 * has_prior_record - 
                           0.4 * has_private_attorney)
        
        plea_accept_prob = pm.Deterministic('plea_acceptance_probability',
                                          pm.math.sigmoid(plea_accept_logit))
        
        # Counterfactual: conviction rate without plea bargains
        counterfactual_conviction = pm.Deterministic('conviction_rate_without_pleas',
                                                   pm.math.sigmoid(conviction_logit - plea_bargain_effect * accepted_plea))
        
        # System efficiency metrics
        case_resolution_time = pm.Deterministic('avg_case_resolution_days',
                                              pm.math.switch(accepted_plea, 45, 180))
        
        system_cost_per_case = pm.Deterministic('system_cost_per_case',
                                              pm.math.switch(accepted_plea, 5000, 25000))
        
        return model
```

prompt: Quantum computing lab modeling error correction for topological qubits. Collected data from 50 error correction cycles on a 7-qubit system. Physical qubit error rates: 0.1% for single-qubit gates, 1.2% for two-qubit gates. Syndrome measurements show: X-stabilizer errors detected 15 times, Z-stabilizer 22 times, Y-type 8 times. Decoherence time T1=45μs, T2=38μs. Logical error rate after correction: 0.015%. Temperature fluctuations between 10-15mK correlate with error bursts. How can we model the error correction threshold and optimize the code distance?
completion:
<think>
Quantum error correction modeling requires:
1. Physical vs logical error rates
2. Stabilizer measurements and syndrome decoding
3. Decoherence effects (T1 relaxation, T2 dephasing)
4. Temperature dependence
5. Threshold theorem for scalability

Key aspects: error propagation, syndrome extraction fidelity, and code distance optimization.
</think>

To model this, I'll employ MCMC sampling for the data structure.

```python
import pymc as pm
import numpy as np

def model(data):
    with pm.Model() as model:
        # Physical error parameters
        p_single = pm.Beta('p_single_qubit_error', alpha=1, beta=999)  # ~0.1%
        p_two = pm.Beta('p_two_qubit_error', alpha=12, beta=988)  # ~1.2%
        
        # Decoherence parameters (in microseconds)
        T1_mean = pm.Gamma('T1_relaxation_time', mu=45, sigma=5)
        T2_mean = pm.Gamma('T2_dephasing_time', mu=38, sigma=4)
        
        # Temperature effect on errors (mK)
        temp_baseline = 12.5  # midpoint
        temp_coefficient = pm.Normal('temp_error_coefficient', mu=0.002, sigma=0.0005)  # per mK
        
        # Syndrome measurement fidelity
        syndrome_fidelity = pm.Beta('syndrome_measurement_fidelity', alpha=95, beta=5)  # ~95%
        
        # Error correction code parameters
        code_distance = data['code_distance']  # 3 for 7-qubit code
        n_qubits = data['n_physical_qubits']
        n_cycles = data['n_correction_cycles']
        
        # Temperature data
        temperature = data['temperature_mK']
        temp_effect = temp_coefficient * (temperature - temp_baseline)
        
        # Time-dependent error rates
        gate_time = 0.1  # microseconds
        measurement_time = 1.0  # microseconds
        
        # Error rates including decoherence
        t_operation = gate_time + measurement_time
        p_T1_error = 1 - pm.math.exp(-t_operation / T1_mean)
        p_T2_error = 1 - pm.math.exp(-t_operation / T2_mean)
        
        # Combined physical error rate
        p_physical = pm.Deterministic('p_physical_combined',
                                     p_single + p_T1_error + p_T2_error + temp_effect)
        
        # Pauli error channel model
        # Probability of X, Y, Z errors
        pauli_probs = pm.Dirichlet('pauli_error_distribution', 
                                   a=[15, 8, 22])  # Based on syndrome observations
        p_X = pauli_probs[0]
        p_Y = pauli_probs[1] 
        p_Z = pauli_probs[2]
        
        # Syndrome extraction error model
        # Each stabilizer measurement can fail
        n_stabilizers = 6  # For 7-qubit Steane code
        p_syndrome_error = 1 - syndrome_fidelity
        
        # Logical error rate model
        # For distance d code, need (d+1)/2 errors to fail
        error_threshold = (code_distance + 1) // 2
        
        # Effective error rate after syndrome measurement
        p_effective = p_physical * (1 + p_syndrome_error)
        
        # Binomial model for number of errors
        n_errors_per_round = pm.Binomial('n_errors_per_round',
                                         n=n_qubits,
                                         p=p_effective)
        
        # Logical error occurs if errors exceed threshold
        p_logical_error = pm.Deterministic('p_logical_error_per_round',
                                          1 - pm.math.sum([
                                              pm.math.exp(pm.distributions.Binomial.logp(
                                                  k, n=n_qubits, p=p_effective))
                                              for k in range(error_threshold)
                                          ]))
        
        # Observed logical errors
        logical_errors_observed = pm.Binomial('logical_errors_observed',
                                             n=n_cycles,
                                             p=p_logical_error,
                                             observed=data['n_logical_errors'])
        
        # Error correction threshold calculation
        # Threshold: p_logical < p_physical for large codes
        threshold_ratio = pm.Deterministic('threshold_ratio',
                                          p_logical_error / p_physical)
        
        # Code distance optimization
        # Model logical error rate vs code distance
        distances = np.array([3, 5, 7, 9])  # Possible code distances
        n_qubits_by_distance = distances ** 2  # Approximate for surface codes
        
        logical_rates_by_distance = []
        for d in distances:
            threshold_d = (d + 1) // 2
            # Rough approximation: p_L ≈ (p_phys)^((d+1)/2)
            p_logical_d = p_effective ** threshold_d
            logical_rates_by_distance.append(p_logical_d)
        
        logical_error_scaling = pm.Deterministic('logical_error_by_distance',
                                               pm.math.stack(logical_rates_by_distance))
        
        # Resource requirements by distance
        resource_scaling = pm.Deterministic('qubits_required_by_distance',
                                          n_qubits_by_distance)
        
        # Error burst modeling
        # Hidden Markov model for temperature-induced bursts
        burst_states = 2  # Normal vs burst
        transition_prob = pm.Beta('burst_transition_prob', alpha=1, beta=99)  # 1% chance
        burst_error_multiplier = pm.Gamma('burst_error_multiplier', mu=5, sigma=1)
        
        # State sequence
        burst_state = pm.Categorical('burst_state',
                                     p=[1-transition_prob, transition_prob],
                                     shape=n_cycles)
        
        # Error rate modulation by burst state
        error_rate_modulated = pm.Deterministic('error_rate_with_bursts',
                                              pm.math.switch(burst_state,
                                                           p_effective * burst_error_multiplier,
                                                           p_effective))
        
        # Syndrome statistics
        syndrome_counts = pm.Multinomial('syndrome_counts',
                                        n=data['total_syndromes'],
                                        p=pauli_probs,
                                        observed=data['syndrome_observations'])
        
        # Quantum process fidelity
        process_fidelity = pm.Deterministic('quantum_process_fidelity',
                                          (1 - p_logical_error) ** n_cycles)
        
        # Optimal code distance selection
        # Balance error rate vs resource cost
        resource_cost_per_qubit = 1000  # Arbitrary units
        
        total_cost_by_distance = []
        for i, d in enumerate(distances):
            error_cost = 10000 * logical_rates_by_distance[i]  # Cost of errors
            resource_cost = resource_cost_per_qubit * n_qubits_by_distance[i]
            total_cost = error_cost + resource_cost
            total_cost_by_distance.append(total_cost)
        
        cost_by_distance = pm.Deterministic('total_cost_by_code_distance',
                                          pm.math.stack(total_cost_by_distance))
        
        optimal_distance_idx = pm.Deterministic('optimal_code_distance_index',
                                              pm.math.argmin(cost_by_distance))
        
        # Decoder performance metrics
        decoder_success_rate = pm.Beta('decoder_success_rate', 
                                      alpha=48, beta=2)  # Based on 48/50 successful corrections
        
        # Time to solution including error correction overhead
        raw_gate_time = 0.1  # μs
        syndrome_extraction_time = 1.0  # μs  
        decoding_time = 5.0  # μs classical processing
        
        ec_cycle_time = syndrome_extraction_time + decoding_time
        overhead_factor = pm.Deterministic('error_correction_overhead',
                                         ec_cycle_time / raw_gate_time)
        
        # Effective computation rate
        logical_gate_rate = pm.Deterministic('logical_gates_per_second',
                                           1e6 / (raw_gate_time * overhead_factor * 
                                                 (1 + p_logical_error)))  # Account for retry
        
        # Temperature control requirements
        # What temperature stability maintains threshold?
        temp_tolerance = pm.Deterministic('temperature_tolerance_mK',
                                        (threshold_ratio * p_physical) / temp_coefficient)
        
        # Crosstalk effects between qubits
        crosstalk_coefficient = pm.HalfNormal('crosstalk_coefficient', sigma=0.001)
        nearest_neighbor_errors = crosstalk_coefficient * p_two
        
        # Full system error budget
        error_budget = pm.Deterministic('error_budget_breakdown',
                                      pm.math.stack([
                                          p_single,  # Gate errors
                                          p_T1_error,  # T1 relaxation
                                          p_T2_error,  # T2 dephasing  
                                          temp_effect,  # Temperature
                                          nearest_neighbor_errors,  # Crosstalk
                                          p_syndrome_error  # Measurement
                                      ]))
        
        # Threshold theorem validation
        # Check if increasing code distance reduces logical error
        threshold_achieved = pm.Deterministic('threshold_theorem_satisfied',
                                            pm.math.lt(logical_error_scaling[1], 
                                                      logical_error_scaling[0]))
        
        return model
```

prompt: SEIR compartmental model for tracking Omicron BA.5 spread through 3 neighboring cities. City A (pop 500K): 2500 cases week 1, 8900 week 2, 23400 week 3. City B (pop 300K): 1200 week 1, 5600 week 2, 18900 week 3. City C (pop 200K): 800 week 1, 3400 week 2, 11200 week 3. Mobility data shows 5% daily commuters A↔B, 2% B↔C, 1% A↔C. Hospital capacity: A=2000 beds, B=1000, C=600. Current occupancy 65%. Vaccination rates: A=72%, B=68%, C=61%. Model R0, peak timing, and intervention effectiveness.
completion:
<think>
SEIR model with spatial structure needed:
1. Three connected cities with mobility
2. Exponential growth phase data
3. Hospital capacity constraints
4. Vaccination providing partial protection
5. Need to estimate R0, predict peaks, evaluate interventions

Key: coupling between cities through commuter flows.
</think>

Here's how we can approach this with informative priors.

```python
import pymc as pm
import numpy as np

def model(data):
    with pm.Model() as model:
        # Population and initial conditions
        pop_sizes = np.array([500000, 300000, 200000])  # Cities A, B, C
        n_cities = 3
        n_weeks = data['n_weeks_observed']
        
        # Vaccination effectiveness against infection
        vax_rates = np.array([0.72, 0.68, 0.61])
        vax_effectiveness = pm.Beta('vaccine_effectiveness', alpha=30, beta=70)  # ~30% vs Omicron
        
        # Effective susceptible population
        S_eff = pop_sizes * (1 - vax_rates * vax_effectiveness)
        
        # Basic reproduction number R0 (city-specific)
        R0_base = pm.Gamma('R0_base', mu=5.0, sigma=0.5)  # Omicron BA.5
        R0_city_effects = pm.Normal('R0_city_effects', mu=0, sigma=0.2, shape=n_cities)
        R0 = pm.Deterministic('R0_by_city', R0_base * pm.math.exp(R0_city_effects))
        
        # SEIR parameters
        incubation_period = pm.Gamma('incubation_period', mu=3.0, sigma=0.5)  # days
        infectious_period = pm.Gamma('infectious_period', mu=5.0, sigma=1.0)  # days
        
        sigma = 1 / incubation_period  # E → I rate
        gamma = 1 / infectious_period  # I → R rate
        beta = R0 * gamma  # Transmission rate
        
        # Mobility matrix (daily commuter fraction)
        mobility = np.array([
            [0.90, 0.05, 0.01],  # From A to A, B, C
            [0.05, 0.88, 0.02],  # From B to A, B, C  
            [0.01, 0.02, 0.95]   # From C to A, B, C
        ])
        
        # Initial conditions from Week 1 data
        I_init = np.array([2500, 1200, 800])
        E_init = I_init * 2  # Assume 2x exposed vs infectious
        R_init = I_init * 10  # Assume some prior immunity
        S_init = S_eff - I_init - E_init - R_init
        
        # Time series storage
        S = np.zeros((n_weeks * 7 + 1, n_cities))  # Daily resolution
        E = np.zeros((n_weeks * 7 + 1, n_cities))
        I = np.zeros((n_weeks * 7 + 1, n_cities))
        R = np.zeros((n_weeks * 7 + 1, n_cities))
        
        S[0] = S_init
        E[0] = E_init
        I[0] = I_init
        R[0] = R_init
        
        # SEIR dynamics with spatial coupling
        dt = 1.0  # 1 day time step
        
        for t in range(n_weeks * 7):
            # Effective populations accounting for mobility
            S_eff_t = mobility.T @ S[t]  # Where susceptibles spend their day
            I_eff_t = mobility.T @ I[t]  # Where infectious spend their day
            
            # Force of infection in each location
            foi = beta[:, None] * I_eff_t / pop_sizes
            
            # New infections by residence
            new_infections = S[t] * (mobility @ foi)
            
            # SEIR transitions
            dS = -new_infections
            dE = new_infections - sigma * E[t]
            dI = sigma * E[t] - gamma * I[t]
            dR = gamma * I[t]
            
            # Update compartments
            S[t+1] = S[t] + dS * dt
            E[t+1] = E[t] + dE * dt
            I[t+1] = I[t] + dI * dt
            R[t+1] = R[t] + dR * dt
        
        # Observation model - weekly reported cases
        # Assume reporting rate and delay
        reporting_rate = pm.Beta('reporting_rate', alpha=40, beta=60)  # ~40%
        reporting_delay = pm.Gamma('reporting_delay_days', mu=3, sigma=1)
        
        # Aggregate to weekly observations
        weekly_cases_model = []
        for week in range(n_weeks):
            start_day = week * 7
            end_day = (week + 1) * 7
            # New infections this week (with reporting delay)
            delay_idx = pm.math.maximum(0, pm.math.floor(reporting_delay))
            week_infections = pm.math.sum(new_infections[start_day:end_day], axis=0)
            reported = week_infections * reporting_rate
            weekly_cases_model.append(reported)
        
        weekly_cases_expected = pm.Deterministic('weekly_cases_expected',
                                                pm.math.stack(weekly_cases_model))
        
        # Observations with negative binomial for overdispersion
        dispersion = pm.Gamma('dispersion', mu=10, sigma=5)
        
        cases_observed = pm.NegativeBinomial('cases_observed',
                                           mu=weekly_cases_expected,
                                           alpha=dispersion,
                                           observed=data['weekly_cases'])
        
        # Hospital demand modeling
        hospitalization_rate = pm.Beta('hospitalization_rate', 
                                      alpha=3, beta=97)  # ~3% for Omicron
        hospital_los = pm.Gamma('hospital_length_of_stay', mu=5, sigma=2)  # days
        
        # Time to hospitalization after infection
        time_to_hosp = pm.Gamma('time_to_hospitalization', mu=7, sigma=2)
        
        # Hospital occupancy projection
        hospital_demand = []
        for t in range(len(I)):
            # Patients are those infected ~7 days ago, staying ~5 days
            hosp_idx = pm.math.maximum(0, t - pm.math.floor(time_to_hosp))
            current_hosp = I[hosp_idx] * hospitalization_rate * \
                          pm.math.minimum(1, hospital_los / infectious_period)
            hospital_demand.append(current_hosp)
        
        hospital_occupancy = pm.Deterministic('hospital_occupancy',
                                            pm.math.stack(hospital_demand))
        
        # Peak timing and magnitude
        peak_infections_day = pm.Deterministic('peak_day_by_city',
                                             pm.math.argmax(I, axis=0))
        peak_infections = pm.Deterministic('peak_infections_by_city',
                                         pm.math.max(I, axis=0))
        
        peak_hospital_day = pm.Deterministic('peak_hospital_day',
                                           pm.math.argmax(hospital_occupancy, axis=0))
        peak_hospital_demand = pm.Deterministic('peak_hospital_demand',
                                              pm.math.max(hospital_occupancy, axis=0))
        
        # Hospital capacity analysis
        hospital_capacity = np.array([2000, 1000, 600])
        baseline_occupancy = 0.65
        available_capacity = hospital_capacity * (1 - baseline_occupancy)
        
        overflow_risk = pm.Deterministic('hospital_overflow_probability',
                                       pm.math.sigmoid(10 * (peak_hospital_demand / 
                                                           available_capacity - 1)))
        
        # Intervention effectiveness modeling
        # 1. Mobility reduction
        mobility_reduction = 0.5  # 50% reduction scenario
        R_eff_mobility = pm.Deterministic('R_eff_with_mobility_reduction',
                                        R0 * (1 - mobility_reduction * 0.6))  # Assuming 60% of transmission is mobile
        
        # 2. Mask mandate  
        mask_effectiveness = pm.Beta('mask_effectiveness', alpha=30, beta=70)  # ~30%
        mask_compliance = pm.Beta('mask_compliance', alpha=60, beta=40)  # ~60%
        R_eff_masks = pm.Deterministic('R_eff_with_masks',
                                     R0 * (1 - mask_effectiveness * mask_compliance))
        
        # 3. Combined interventions
        R_eff_combined = pm.Deterministic('R_eff_combined_interventions',
                                        R0 * (1 - mobility_reduction * 0.6) * 
                                        (1 - mask_effectiveness * mask_compliance))
        
        # Attack rate (final size)
        # Approximate using final size equation
        def final_size_equation(R0_val):
            # Newton's method for solving: log(1-z) + R0*z = 0
            z = 0.5  # Initial guess
            for _ in range(10):
                f = pm.math.log(1 - z) + R0_val * z
                f_prime = -1/(1-z) + R0_val
                z = z - f / f_prime
            return z
        
        attack_rate_no_intervention = pm.Deterministic('attack_rate_baseline',
                                                     final_size_equation(R0))
        attack_rate_with_intervention = pm.Deterministic('attack_rate_with_interventions',
```
